event_comments:
Lake Wilderness Park is a mix of open spaces, ballfields, and forested trails; in particular, the tight network of trails in the arboretum can cause confusion. When traversing the open areas, please avoid disrupting any teams practicing or playing a game. Additionally, the mixture of open areas and forested “walls” in and around the frisbee golf course makes for some interesting navigation choices and challenges. Please be courteous to golfers and please watch for flying discs, as the course is very popular and will not be closed during our event. The swimming beach and beach house will be open for those that want to take a quick cool-down swim afterwards!
The third event in the 2019 Wednesday Evening Series will be held at Lake Wilderness Park! The Wednesday Evening Series is our most relaxed, casual set of navigation events, held in local parks once the weather warms up. These events are a little more low-key than those in the Winter League, and we typically offer a barbeque afterward for all participants. Decompress after work and bring a coworker or two along. Or meet your family at the park and enjoy a night off from cooking. Or meet a bunch of friends at the park for an evening of exploring together, chatting over some food, and finishing up with frisbee on the lawn.
Events in the Wednesday Evening Series are generally less challenging (both physically and navigationally) than many of our other events, and are therefore great for beginners and those less experienced with orienteering – while also offering a challenge to longtime participants of the sport.
